source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Ship Shoal Block 177 Platform A Fire

actions_taken: Fire Watch immediately extinguished the fire with a portable fire extinguisher
BSEE Inspectors conducted investigation on August 7, 2017
Documentation collected and SSV diaphragm leak identified
event.summary: On August 2, 2017, a fire occurred on Ship Shoal Block 177 Platform A during decommissioning operations of well A4. A cutting crew was removing a casing valve using a cutting torch when a fire ignited at a weep hole on well A5's Surface Safety Valve (SSV). The fire was extinguished in approximately 3 seconds by the Fire Watch using a portable fire extinguisher. Investigation revealed that well A5 had a leaking diaphragm in its SSV which allowed gas to escape and be ignited by slag from the cutting torch. The primary cause was the failure to shut-in well A5 prior to commencing hot work, compounded by lack of communication between the cutting crew and the Production Operator.
recommendations: Ensure clear communication protocols between all personnel involved in hot work operations, particularly between cutting crews and production operators responsible for well shut-in
Reconcile conflicting procedures between Job Safety Environmental Analysis (JSEA) and Hot Work Permits to ensure consistency in hazard identification and control requirements
Implement verification procedures to confirm well shut-in status prior to hot work commencement, with documented sign-off from the responsible Production Operator
key_phrases: leaking diaphragm in Surface Safety Valve
gas ignition from cutting torch slag

hazards.name: Pressurized hydrocarbon gas in well A5
Hot work operations (cutting torch) in well bay

threats.name: Well A5 not shut-in prior to hot work
Gas leakage from SSV diaphragm undetected by gas detector
Ignition source (cutting torch slag) in proximity to leaking gas

consequences.name: Fire at Surface Safety Valve weep hole

controls.name: Well shut-in procedure prior to hot work
controls.barrier_role: Prevent pressurized gas from being present during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to eliminate hazard source

supporting_text: The JSEA identified that well A5 was flowing and needed to be shut-in prior to hot work commencing
The primary cause of this incident was the failure to shut-in well A5 prior to commencing hot work

The Production Operator was the only personnel on the facility with the knowledge to ensure the well was shut-in, and he was not present when the hot work started nor was he consulted with to ensure the well was shut-in

controls.name: Job Safety Environmental Analysis (JSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and required mitigations for hot work

supporting_text: A Job Safety Environmental Analysis (JSEA) and a Hot Work Permit were created to identify the job steps, potential hazards, and hazard mitigations
The JSEA identified that well A5 was flowing and needed to be shut-in prior to hot work commencing, and was signed by the Welder, Fire Watch, Production Operator, and Company Representative

controls.name: Hot Work Permit
controls.barrier_role: Authorize and control hot work operations with hazard mitigations

supporting_text: The Hot Work Permit had Not Applicable (N/A) checked off next to the requirements to shut-in flowing wells, redirect flowing wells, and/or protect flowing wells from hot work operations
Conflicting procedures: The JSEA and the hot work permit conflicted. The JSEA stated that well A5 needed to be shut-in, but the hot work permit had N/A next to the requirement to shut-in the well

controls.name: Gas detection and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect presence of flammable gas in work area

supporting_text: The Fire Watch had a gas detector and was taking samples in the area of the hot work, but the SSV was a few feet higher than where the cutting was taking place
The gas that was leaking past the diaphragm was a very small amount and was not reading on the Fire Watch's gas detector

controls.name: Fire Watch with portable fire extinguisher
controls.barrier_role: Detect and suppress fire immediately upon ignition

supporting_text: The Fire Watch immediately extinguished the fire with a portable fire extinguisher, and the duration of the fire was approximately 3 seconds
There was no damage identified, paint in the area of the weep hole was not scorched or discolored

controls.name: Surface Safety Valve (SSV) with diaphragm
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas flow from well when shut-in

supporting_text: It was discovered that the SSV for well A5 had a leaking diaphragm, which allowed gas to be ignited by the slag from the cutting torch
Well A5 had a leaking diaphragm in its SSV, but if the well had been shut-in the hazard would not have existed

controls.name: Communication protocol between cutting crew and Production Operator
controls.barrier_role: Ensure Production Operator confirms well shut-in status before hot work

supporting_text: Lack of Communication: The cutting crew and Company Representative failed to communicate with the Production Operator to ensure well A5 was shut-in

The Welder and Fire Watch both stated that they were informed by the Company Representative and others that well A5 was already shut-in so they did not think they needed to talk to the Production Operator
The Production Operator confirmed that the cutting crew never requested him to shut-in A5

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
